metadata_information.title: The macroeconomics of price reform in socialist countries : a dynamic framework
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: The macroeconomics of price reform in socialist countries : a dynamic framework

document_description.abstract: This paper emphasizes the fiscal underpinnings of the inflationary process and those particular dynamics when a dual price system is present. In particular, it explores the links between price controls and decontrols and the government budget, mainly through the flow of subsidies to either consumers or producers. A clear conclusion is that without consistency in macroeconomic policy, price liberalization may simply exacerbate imbalances and ultimately provide a mechanism for sustaining inflation, hence compromising a basic objective of macroeconomic policy.
